methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
DustPhotonics is an acquired-stage company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, founded by Ronnie Cohen and Arik Sandler. The company has raised $75M+. The sector comprises a modest comp universe of 3 public companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Standard valuation triangulation methods face material constraints here. Peer-Median analysis is inapplicable: while 14 same-sector private peers exist, none satisfy narrow stage and tier filters simultaneously, leaving fewer than three datapoints for any cohort. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot proceed because DustPhotonics' revenue remains undisclosed—standard for private companies—and applying the 22.3x P/S median observed across the three public semiconductors comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) would be unfounded without this input.

The absence of applicable quantitative methods means no supported valuation range can be derived from this dataset. Any estimate would require either: (1) target revenue disclosure to anchor public multiples, or (2) a sufficiently dense set of contemporaneous private transactions at similar stage and scale within semiconductors. Until such data surfaces, valuation remains indeterminate from available sources. The acquisition itself implies third-party price discovery, but that consideration falls outside the analytical scope provided.
